title: dichanthelium acuminatum subsp. thermale
intro: Dichanthelium acuminatum subsp. thermale, also known as thermal panic grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial plant native to North America, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Dichanthelium acuminatum subsp. thermale include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
